programme.title: ...Sings Dylan
programme.desc: A look through the archives at some of the many artists who have come into BBC studios to sing their versions of Bob Dylan songs. Featured performers include Peter, Paul and Mary, Lulu, The Byrds, Joan Baez, Eric Clapton, Madeleine Peyroux, Bryan Ferry, UB40, Julie Felix, Manfred Mann, The Brian Auger Trinity and Pops Staples.

programme.title: ...Sings Dylan II
programme.desc: A feast of cover versions of Bob Dylan songs from the BBC archives, with classic tracks old and new and some surprises from the 1960s to the present. From the essential folk queen Joan Baez to punk princess Siouxsie and the Banshees, from The Hollies to Adele, taking in the likes of Julie Felix, Richie Havens, Bryan Ferry and KT Tunstall along the way, the programme reflects Dylan's long career of writing extraordinary songs and the fascination of other artists with them. Peter, Paul and Mary's sublime The Times They Are A-Changin' rubs shoulders with the close harmony of Cliff Richard and The Nolan Sisters' smooth interpretation of the protest classic Blowin' in the Wind. The Blues Band's energetic 1980s updating of Maggie's Farm contrasts with Tom Jones's powerful rootsy What Good Am I? A treat for the Dylan fan and the Dylan novice alike.

programme.title: Bob Dylan: Shadow Kingdom
programme.desc: A showcase of Bob Dylan with his band in an intimate setting as he performs songs from his extensive body of work, including Forever Young, I'll Be Your Baby Tonight, It's All Over Now Baby Blue and many more. This film marks Bob Dylan's first concert since 2019 and the first performance since his acclaimed album Rough and Rowdy Ways.

programme.title: Tangled Up with Dylan: The Ballad of AJ Weberman
programme.desc: Documentary chronicling the life, times and crimes of Bob Dylan obsessive AJ Weberman, who spent years rooting through Dylan's rubbish to try to gain insight into his life.

programme.title: Top of the Pops
programme.desc: Jamie Theakston presents the pop chart programme, first broadcast on 5 November 1999 and featuring Macy Gray, Phil Collins, Diana Ross, Ian Brown, Mariah Carey, Semisonic, Ricky Martin and Five.

programme.desc: Jayne Middlemiss presents the pop chart programme, first broadcast on 12 November 1999 and featuring Tin Tin Out feat Emma Bunton, Savage Garden, Beck, Another Level, Jennifer Lopez, Mr Vegas, Children's Promise and Geri Halliwell.

programme.desc: Leonard Sachs presents an edition of the old time music hall programme, first broadcast on 24 February 1977. With Larry Grayson, Fenella Fielding, Hinge and Bracket, Anna Sharkey and members of the Players' Theatre, London.

programme.title: Dusty
programme.desc: Dusty Springfield's 1960s variety show, first broadcast on 22 September 1966, with the voices of Madeline Bell, Lesley Duncan and Margaret Stredder. Her special guest is ventriloquist Señor Wences.

programme.title: Hetty Wainthropp Investigates
programme.desc: Drama series about a 60-year-old private detective. Hetty is in hospital - an unlikely place to find her most urgent case yet.

programme.title: My Favourite Cake
programme.desc: One night. Two lonely hearts. Endless Possibilities. Seventy-year-old Mahin lives alone in Tehran since her husband's death and her daughter's departure for Europe, until an afternoon tea with friends leads her to break her solitary routine and revitalise her love life. But as Mahin opens herself up to romance, what begins as an unexpected encounter quickly evolves into an unpredictable, unforgettable evening. The film explores themes of love, regret and second chances. It’s set against the backdrop of a country with strict rules and restrictions, especially when it comes to the rights and freedoms of women. In Farsi with English subtitles.

programme.title: King Otto and Football’s Greek Gods
programme.desc: In the summer of 2004, audiences looked on in disbelief as the Greek national football team, who had never previously won a single match or even scored a goal in a major tournament, took down the giants of world football to become the unlikeliest of European champions. The architect behind this unprecedented triumph was legendary German football coach ‘King’ Otto Rehhagel. After accomplishing every major success in Germany, he made the bold decision to leave all he knew behind and work in a foreign country with the underachieving Greek national team. This is the story of how these two contrasting cultures came together to speak the same language and write a new chapter of Greek mythology.

programme.title: Keeping Up Appearances
programme.desc: Hyacinth finds herself with an ideal opportunity to impress Emmet with her singing when she discovers that he is involved in a concert at the church hall. However, Richard has other things on his mind, dreading the thought of early retirement and having to spend more time with his wife.

programme.title: Sorry!
programme.desc: Timothy, after some initial complications, is asked to be best man at his friend Frank's wedding. He ends up in trouble as a result of trying to encourage the idea of marriage in spite of his parents.

programme.title: The Good Old Days
programme.desc: Leonard Sachs presents an edition of the old time music hall programme, first broadcast on 24 February 1977. With Larry Grayson, Fenella Fielding, Hinge and Bracket, Anna Sharkey and members of the Players' Theatre, London.

programme.title: Pubs, Ponds and Power: The Story of the Village
programme.desc: Archaeologist Ben Robinson explores the Cornish fishing village of Port Isaac. Behind the quaint facade lies something far more gritty - a place where people exploited a range of natural resources, on land and at sea, to make a living and find profits far beyond Britain's shores.

programme.title: Afoot Again in the Past
programme.desc: Kirsty Wark visits the island of Inchmahome in Scotland, haven to Mary Queen of Scots, and ruminates on what it means to be Scottish.

programme.title: Crooks Anonymous
programme.desc: Dandy Forsdyke, a habitual criminal, is engaged to Babette, who wants him to go straight. Initiated into Crooks Anonymous, Forsdyke is guided to give up his criminal ways, but temptations abound.

programme.title: Being Stanley Baxter
programme.desc: TV and panto star Stanley Baxter had big success but an even bigger secret. With access to close family and friends, personal archive and recordings, we reveal his true story.

programme.title: The Magic of Dance
programme.desc: Margot Fonteyn traces the story of the Romantic ballet and its greatest exponents. In 1832, the most famous ballerina in the world was Marie Taglioni. One of the first ballerinas to dance on the tips of her toes, she epitomised the Romantic age - ethereal and sylph-like. Her most famous role was La Sylphide. By mid-century, however, there were new styles, and it was left to the Russians to revive the Romantic age in 1909, when they brought Les Sylphides to Paris.

programme.title: Les Sylphides
programme.desc: A studio performance of the romantic ballet Les Sylphides, first broadcast on 3 April 1953 and starring Alicia Markova, John Field, Violetta Elvin and Svetlana Beriosova. Created by Michel Fokine and set to music by Frédéric Chopin, the ballet premiered in 1909 and was unusual at the time for being non-narrative, where grace and movement carry the piece rather than a storyline. The programme is introduced by renowned Russian prima ballerina Tamara Karsavina, who had danced in the first performance of the ballet in Paris on 2 June 1909. Les Sylphides is the first complete ballet in the BBC archive and was recorded by pointing a film camera at a television screen and filming the result (a method used for all recording until the arrival of video tape in the 1960s).

programme.title: Omnibus
programme.desc: Theatre and film director Lindsay Anderson in conversation with ballet superstar Rudolf Nureyev. First broadcast in 1974.

programme.title: The Classical Collection
programme.desc: The natural world has always been a powerful inspiration to composers. From vast forests and tiny fish to wild storms and epic seascapes, this programme takes us on an evocative journey through some of the best-loved musical responses to our living planet.

programme.title: BBC News
programme.desc: The latest news from the BBC.

programme.title: The Media Show
programme.desc: Munya Chawawa found viral fame during the lockdown for his satirical videos on social media. Now he has a new Channel 4 documentary, Wrestling with Trump, which looks at the links between professional wrestling and the MAGA movement. Restrictions on prisoners in the UK speaking to journalists are in focus after the Guardian was told Jeremy Bamber can no longer exchange letters or phone calls with journalists. Bamber was convicted in 1986 of murdering his adoptive family and is serving life in prison. Simon Hattenstone, feature writer at the Guardian, and Emily Bolton, defence lawyer and founder of Objection, set out the issues. The top parenting influencers, often dubbed as “momfluencers”, can earn millions. The phenomena is the subject of a new book, Like, Follow, Subscribe: Influencer Kids and the Cost of a Childhood Online. Its author, Fortesa Latifi, and Jasmine McInnes, family and travel influencer, discuss the world of “sharenting”.

programme.title: Rachel Khoo's Kitchen Notebook
programme.desc: Rachel indulges her passion for the Swedish outdoors, inspired by both the woodland and the water. Whilst visiting the Swedish Archipelago, Rachel shows off her fishing skills and gets into the Scandinavian way of life cooking a perch-style wrap on an open fire at the waterside by a traditional Swedish cabin. Back home, Rachel recreates Swedish favourites with her very own Khoo twist. She makes a savoury Swedish celebration cake, known as smörgåstårta, a wild mushroom soup with dill fish balls and an edible forest floor.

programme.title: The Travel Show
programme.desc: It's an important year for the city of Hull, in northern England. It's celebrating more than 800 years of maritime history with a raft of new museum openings that it's hoped will make it a world-class visitor attraction. Emeline Nsingi Nkosi goes to see the opening of the restored Spurn Lightship - literally a lighthouse on a ship - which was a mainstay on the waterways there for 50 years. She meets the people involved in the huge regeneration effort and finds out why the city's been the punchline of jokes for so many years.

programme.title: Talking Movies
programme.desc: For Talking Movies presenter Tom Brook, April 2026 marks a momentous milestone. It will be the 50th anniversary of Tom joining the BBC as a trainee news journalist. Over the decades, from London, Berlin and mostly New York, he has reported on culture and cinema in thousands of stories and programmes broadcast on the BBC. Tom has interviewed countless leading figures in cinema, from early pioneers like Bette Davis and Jimmy Cagney up to contemporary star names, including Timothee Chalamet, Meryl Streep and Lady Gaga. Tom has also met US presidents and UK prime ministers, and leaders in global culture from Bollywood to LA. In a special edition of Talking Movies, he looks back at his career and the actors and filmmakers he has encountered over 50 years. He reflects on how the film industry has changed over the last five decades and how, in very tumultuous times, he believes cinema is more relevant and useful to our lives than ever before.

programme.title: BBC News

programme.title: Unspun World with John Simpson

programme.desc: AI Decoded examines a dramatic week for Elon Musk that could reshape the future of artificial intelligence. A US court has rejected Musk’s claims against OpenAI, bringing a long-running feud with co-founder Sam Altman into sharp focus and offering a rare glimpse inside one of the world’s most influential AI companies. At the same time, SpaceX is preparing for what could be the largest stock market listing in history, a move that may value the company at up to $1.75tn and make Musk the world's first human trillionaire.

programme.title: Morning Live
programme.desc: Join Gethin Jones, Helen Skelton, Michelle Ackerley and the team for conversation, advice and tips to make your day that little bit easier.

programme.title: Rip Off Britain
programme.desc: Gloria Hunniford, Julia Somerville and Louise Minchin come to the aid of people stuck with paying a monthly contract for expensive smartphones that were never delivered - and their phone companies don’t seem to want to help. Also on the programme, the drivers left fearing for their families’ safety when expensive SUVs developed a power-steering fault.

programme.title: Homes Under the Hammer
programme.desc: It's a labour of love for two brothers who create a wonderful home for their sister in Chesterton, while Martin is puzzled by a door leading nowhere in Plymouth and Dion follows a speedy flat renovation in Wales.

programme.title: Bargain Hunt
programme.desc: If there was ever an episode for animal lovers, it’s this one! Christina presents a show all about our furry, feathered and four-legged friends, and looks back at the teams who have sniffed out some tail-wagging treasures. Expect cuddly cats, delightful dogs and even a few perky pigs as Christina visits an RSPCA centre in Hertfordshire. She counts down the most profitable pet-related items purchased by red and blues teams at antiques fairs across the country. In Kent, a statue of a golden retriever turns the head of Jenny, who runs a doggy display team; in Carmarthenshire, a couple hope to reel in a good deal with a french-glass fish; and in Nottinghamshire, a silver metal spider brooch gives best friends Pam and Diane the creeps! Away from the shopping, Eric learns about the origin of Nipper, the iconic HMV dog, and Charlie’s off the leash, learning about antique dog collars.

programme.title: BBC News at One
programme.desc: The latest national and international news from the BBC.

programme.title: RHS Chelsea Flower Show
programme.desc: Celebrating a fantastic week at the RHS Chelsea Flower Show 2026, Nicki Chapman and Angellica Bell are joined by Dame Mary Berry to reveal which garden has won the BBC RHS People’s Choice Award. Florist to the stars Hamish Powell shows you how to create fabulous floral displays, and we answer more of your questions in the Chelsea Garden Clinic.

programme.title: Escape to the Country
programme.desc: Having met online and fallen in love just over a year ago, today's couple are calling on the help of Nicki Chapman to find them their very first home together in the East Sussex countryside. To better understand what living in this county is like, Nicki meets up with one of its residents, none other than fellow Escape to the Country presenter Alistair Appleton. Alistair welcomes Nicki to his beautiful, off-grid, 200-year-old former coastguard’s cottage, which he calls home.

programme.title: The Repair Shop on the Road
programme.desc: The team restore some Victorian stage machinery, Suzie hears a story of injustice from apartheid South Africa, and wood lover Will gets to grips with the delicate art of marquetry.

programme.title: Bridge of Lies
programme.desc: Ross Kemp hosts as a team of celebrity comedians, featuring Susan Calman, John Thomson, Jon Culshaw and Shazia Mirza, takes on the Bridge of Lies for their chosen charities. One by one, they all face the Bridge, choosing who faces each crossing depending on the question category. Each Bridge crossing has 32 potential steps, including 22 truths and ten lies. They must cross the Bridge by stepping on the truths and avoiding the lies, earning money for each correct answer. Step on a lie, and the Bridge will halve their money. Step on three lies, and they are out of the game. There is always a safe path across the Bridge. They just have to find it. Once they have each faced the Bridge on their own, those who have been successful will join forces to face the final crossing for a chance to take home the cash.

programme.title: Pointless
programme.desc: Alexander Armstrong is joined by co-host Susan Wokoma for the brainy quiz where the contestants try to score as few points as possible by plumbing the depths of their general knowledge to come up with the answers no-one else could think of.

programme.title: BBC News at Six
programme.desc: The latest national and international news from the BBC.

programme.title: BBC London
programme.desc: The latest news, sport and weather from London.

programme.title: RHS Chelsea Flower Show
programme.desc: Sophie Raworth and Adam Frost are joined by special guest Dame Mary Berry, who is on a mission to discover more about growing peonies, one of her favourite flowers. Arit Anderson reveals the winner of Best Houseplant Studio, while Adam Frost and JJ Chalmers uncover all the latest trends to emerge from the show this year.

programme.title: The Marvellous Miniature Workshop
programme.desc: Sara Cox and miniaturist Lee Robinson bring Silverwood Colliery back to life in miniature for ex-miner Reg and miner’s son Neil. From the iconic winding wheel to the miners’ banner, every detail recalls the hardships, camaraderie and unforgettable stories of the 1984 strike. Can the model capture the spirit of a community that endured so much?

programme.title: MasterChef
programme.desc: Knockout week is coming to an end, but not before a few more cooks have to say goodbye. Ten contestants remain, and there is only room in the semi-finals for eight.

programme.title: Have I Got News for You
programme.desc: Alexander Armstrong hosts, with guest panellists Phil Wang and Judi Love joining team captains Ian and Paul as they delve into the news.

programme.title: Mrs Brown's Boys
programme.desc: There’s trouble brewing in Finglas as Grandad drops a bombshell – he’s planning to leave! Agnes, of course, is having none of it and immediately goes into detective mode to find out what’s really going on. Meanwhile, Foley’s Bar prepares for its big annual talent show, and Winnie's determined to take part (whether the bar wants her to or not). Between Grandad’s secrets and some highly questionable 'talent', it’s another week of unpredictable mayhem for Agnes and her long-suffering clan.

programme.title: BBC News at Ten
programme.desc: The latest national and international news, with reports from BBC correspondents worldwide.

programme.title: BBC London
programme.desc: The latest news, sport and weather from London.

programme.title: Radio 1’s Big Weekend
programme.desc: Radio 1’s Big Weekend returns to Sunderland’s Herrington Country Park for the first time since 2005, with 100 acts set to perform across three days of live music. Kicking off the festival as Friday night’s Main Stage headliner is the ultimate party starter, Fatboy Slim. The man who helped take dance music from club floors to festival main stages worldwide is set to bring his legendary show to the north east for a high-energy, hands-in-the-air frenzy. With what promises to be a blockbuster set, expect crowd favourites and his trademark barefoot style - this is the ultimate Friday night Dance Party.

programme.title: St Denis Medical
programme.desc: Alex accidentally donates $1,000 for Parker's dog's operation. A man wants to anonymously donate a kidney to his brother.

programme.title: St Denis Medical
programme.desc: Joyce is practising making quick decisions for a change. Sanderson goes too far to get Joyce’s attention. And the nurses threaten a walkout over pay and conditions.

programme.title: Smoggie Queens
programme.desc: There’s no place like home when Dickie takes his work mates on a Wizard of Oz night out.

programme.title: Smoggie Queens
programme.desc: Mam is managing a team for a local charity football match, where Dickie and Lucinda compete to become the best wags!

programme.title: Saturday Kitchen
programme.desc: Matt Tebbutt is joined by chefs Tom Parker Bowles and Tony Singh, and by special guest Natalie Cassidy. Drinks expert Helen McGinn picks the wines to go with the studio dishes. There are also great moments from the BBC food archive.

programme.title: Anna Haugh’s Big Irish Food Tour
programme.desc: Comedian Katherine Ryan takes Anna to the foodie capital of Ireland. Katherine’s memories of her grandmother’s over-boiled and under-seasoned meals have left her with a bad impression of Irish food, but Anna is on a mission to change that. First stop is the world-renowned Ballymaloe Cookery School, somewhere that inspired Anna's own culinary journey, followed by a visit to one of the oldest food markets in Europe, the English Market. Will this change Katherine's mind?

programme.title: Homes Under the Hammer
programme.desc: The mould in a Hampshire house has Martin singing the blues, Martel needs to watch her step in County Durham, and Dion goes wild in Warrington in an overgrown garden.

programme.title: BBC Weekend News
programme.desc: The latest national and international news from the BBC.

programme.title: BBC Weather
programme.desc: The latest weather forecast.

programme.title: Bargain Hunt
programme.desc: Natasha Raskin Sharp and the teams go antiques-hunting in Nottinghamshire with experts Ben Cooper and Izzie Balmer, before heading to Derby for today’s auction. Natasha visits the International Bomber Command Centre in Lincoln to learn of an Allied mission to save nearly one million Dutch lives.

programme.title: Escape to the Country
programme.desc: Briony May Williams is property-shopping in rural Herefordshire, helping an excited couple who are looking to buy their very first home together where they can finally get their enormous collection of potted plants in the ground. They are delighted by the property options presented to them, and a surprise phone call at the end of the search has everyone holding their breath! Briony also meets another couple who developed a natural cider business after they made their dream country escape in search of a more self-sufficient lifestyle.

programme.title: Money for Nothing
programme.desc: They say don't walk under a ladder... Jacqui does more than that, and designer Ayo unlocks the potential of a £2 table with a bold design.

programme.title: Garden Rescue
programme.desc: Helen and Kevin are nearing full retirement and have a garden dominated by three things – a patio that looks too big for the space, a breeze-block wall that looks ugly and a shed that sticks out like a sore thumb. Inspired by Portugal's granite stone sets and raised boardwalks, they like the idea of a low-maintenance seaside garden with little or no lawn and drought-resistant plants like thistles and grasses. With a budget of £4,000, Charlie and Lee have a challenge ahead to craft this coastal oasis despite being 100 miles from the sea.

programme.title: Shrek
programme.desc: Animated comedy. In a land of living fairy tales and nursery rhymes, grumpy ogre Shrek, along with the loudmouthed Donkey, joins beautiful but wilful Princess Fiona in tackling Lord Farquaad's evil schemes to exile the exotic from his lands and wed the unwilling princess.

programme.title: Bridge of Lies
programme.desc: Ross Kemp presents this high-stakes quiz, joined by a celebrity team featuring rugby player and podcaster Joe Marler, actor Jennie McAlpine, comedian Geoff Norcott and presenter Jean Johannson as they take on the Bridge of Lies in support of their chosen charities. One by one, they all face the Bridge, choosing who faces each crossing depending on the question category. Each Bridge crossing has 32 potential steps, including 22 truths and ten lies. They must cross the Bridge by stepping on the truths and avoiding the lies, earning money for each correct answer. Step on a lie and the Bridge will halve their money. Step on three lies, and they are out of the game. There is always a safe path across the Bridge. They just have to find it. Once they have each faced the Bridge on their own, those who have been successful will join forces to face the final crossing for a chance to take home the cash.

programme.title: Blankety Blank
programme.desc: The legendary Blankety Blank is back! Bradley Walsh hosts as a panel of six celebrities fill in the missing blanks and help win brilliant prizes for some very lucky contestants. This week, he’s joined by Jonathan Ross, Joanna Page, Harry Redknapp, Joe Swash, Susan Wokoma and Shazia Mirza.

programme.title: Michael McIntyre's Big Show
programme.desc: Bafta award-winning comedian Michael McIntyre returns with a brand new episode of his Big Show, for an unmissable night of family entertainment filmed at London's prestigious Theatre Royal Drury Lane. TV icon Alison Hammond is rudely awoken in a hilarious edition of the Midnight Gameshow. Kaiser Chiefs frontman Ricky Wilson is given the shock of his life as he becomes the Unexpected Star... Star of the Show. Pop sensation Anne-Marie hands over her phone for a Send to All with serious consequences, and James Blunt performs.

programme.title: Casualty
programme.desc: Stevie reels to discover Matty covered for her mistake.

programme.title: Two Weeks in August
programme.desc: Zoe and her family arrive on a remote Greek island, hoping a holiday reunion with friends will be an escape from reality. But can she keep pretending when her husband Dan’s dark secret threatens to ruin everything? A hedonistic first night party is the release they all need. But it sets off a chain of events that will change them all forever. Zoe begins to wonder if there are bigger forces at play. We are in Greece, after all, land of the gods.

programme.title: Two Weeks in August
programme.desc: On an ill-fated group boat trip, Zoe tries to pretend last night’s betrayal didn’t mean anything. But Dan’s behaviour threatens to push Zoe over the edge. Nat uses the kiss to drive a wedge between Jacob and his boyfriend Will, who has shown up uninvited. Solomon is the only one who doesn’t know what happened last night - does Zoe tell him and destroy two marriages? Jess is forced to take drastic action to avoid confrontation.

programme.title: Radio 1’s Big Weekend
programme.desc: Bringing her anthemic sound to Sunderland is Sweden’s pop phenomenon Zara Larsson, who is fast becoming a Radio 1’s Big Weekend mainstay as she prepares to make her fourth appearance at the festival. Since making her debut in 2017, the then-teenage sensation has grown into a global force and takes to the Main Stage to top the bill for the very first time. Fresh from releasing her latest album, Midnight Sun, Zara is set to light up Herrington Country Park with huge singalong hits and powerhouse vocals that have made her a festival favourite. Expect a standout Saturday night in north east England as the Swedish hit-maker delivers her most anticipated Big Weekend set yet.

programme.title: Radio 1’s Big Weekend
programme.desc: Radio 1’s Big Weekend returns to Sunderland’s Herrington Country Park for a bank holiday weekend packed with live music. Saturday’s line-up promises huge performances from some of the world’s biggest stars, and we relive all the action from across the Main Stage and New Music Stage.

programme.title: Pentecost
programme.desc: A celebration of Pentecost, exploring how people can respond to the call of the Holy Spirit in different ways to transform self and community. In a service from Hope Street Church in the city centre of Wrexham, north Wales, members of the congregation share inspirational stories of finding their faith alongside reflections of how this new and growing church is playing a part in local community regeneration. The service is led by Andy and Rachel Kitchen, with music from Hope Street’s worship band.

programme.title: Homes Under the Hammer
programme.desc: A young couple buy their first home together in Haydock and transform the three-bedroom semi-detached house into a stunning place for them to live. A developer in Blackpool restores a fire-damaged house back to a home for the rental market. And a family create a fantastic four-bedroom house from a small cottage in a picturesque Norfolk village.

programme.title: Football Focus
programme.desc: After 52 years, the longest-running football magazine show in the world comes to an end. In this final edition, we preview the decisive last day of the Premier League, while reminiscing about Football Focus down the years with the help of some old friends.

programme.title: BBC Weekend News
programme.desc: The latest national and international news from the BBC.

programme.title: Weather for the Week Ahead
programme.desc: Detailed weather forecast.

programme.title: Songs of Praise
programme.desc: Aled Jones is in Bristol to explore the spiritual inspiration of John and Charles Wesley, and to introduce classic Wesley hymns from the oldest Methodist building in the world.

programme.title: Points of View
programme.desc: Viewers have their say on the BBC’s television shows. Email your thoughts to pov@bbc.co.uk

programme.title: Lifeline
programme.desc: Ben Fogle presents an appeal on behalf of Hearing Dogs for Deaf People, a charity that provides support as well as trained assistance dogs to help those living with deafness or hearing loss in the UK.

programme.title: Escape to the Country
programme.desc: Jules Hudson is in Perth and Kinross, helping a young couple with a budget of £350,000 to find an ideal country home for them and their two young sons. Jules also meets a group of keen stand-up paddleboarders who do their bit to keep the waterways free of litter.

programme.title: The Americas
programme.desc: The Wild West, where survival demands grit and adaptability. In snowbound Yellowstone, coyotes use skill and geothermal heat to survive. In Colorado, hundreds of rattlesnakes emerge after winter, while a pregnant female risks starvation to give birth. In California, acorn woodpeckers battle for the best food storage sites, using barns and trees to secure their winter supplies. In Arizona’s desert, honeypot ants survive extreme heat by storing nectar inside their own bodies deep underground. On the Great Plains, ageing bison face fierce rivals to win mates, while fires renew the grasslands. In Kansas, wild horses run through lightning-charged skies. On Nebraska’s Platte River, half a million sandhill cranes converge, their courtship dances creating a rare natural spectacle, now captured from above. The Wild West remains a place of extremes, where each creature must fight to endure its challenges.

programme.title: Minions: The Rise of Gru
programme.desc: In 1976, 11-year-old Gru dreams of becoming a supervillain. Ecstatic when he receives a message from the supervillain team the Vicious 6, Gru finds himself the object of their wrath and needs all his wits and help from his minions to escape their wicked clutches.

programme.title: BBC Weekend News
programme.desc: The latest national and international news from the BBC.

programme.title: BBC London
programme.desc: The latest news, sport and weather from London.

programme.title: BBC Weather
programme.desc: The latest weather forecast.

programme.title: Final Score
programme.desc: Jason Mohammad and guests bring you all the goals, latest drama, analysis, reaction and interviews as the Premier League season concludes with plenty still to play for.

programme.title: RHS Chelsea Flower Show
programme.desc: Sophie Raworth and Adam Frost look back at the highlights of the RHS Chelsea Flower Show 2026. They showcase the must-have plants, the award-winning gardens, the latest design trends for your garden, and the famous faces that have visited the world’s greatest gardening event this year.

programme.title: Antiques Roadshow
programme.desc: The roadshow is at Firstsite contemporary art gallery in Colchester for its final visit, where finds include a creepy goblin figurine, a 17th-century amulet and porcelain plaque discovered buried in a box bought at auction. Clive Farahar finds a collection of signed Tolkien books dedicated to a man whose relatives were neighbours of the author, while Mark Hill is challenged to settle a family argument over the authenticity of a bronze figurine. The guitar responsible for the distinctive sound of British bands in the 1960s delights pop culture expert Claire Tole-Moir, and Ronnie Archer Morgan is captivated by a Congolese wooden shield that was previously used to pay off a debt. Siobhan Tyrrell explores a collection of items from the YMCA that reveal the bravery of female volunteers during the First World War while Will Farmer is thrilled to meet the owner of an exceptional Hans Coper vase, originally gifted by the celebrated ceramist himself. Alexandra Gill discovers a painting that may or may not be by the renowned equestrian painter Sir Alfred Munnings, while Fiona Bruce is joined by Lee Young to learn about state-of-the-art techniques in ceramic repairs.

programme.title: Death Valley
programme.desc: Following the death of a chef in an arson attack on his restaurant, John and Janie come to a small fishing village to scrutinise the locals.

programme.title: Dear England
programme.desc: With the England team at an all-time low after an embarrassing defeat to Iceland, Gareth Southgate becomes the new manager. Haunted by his own penalty miss with England, he inherits a broken team and a jaded fanbase. With psychologist Pippa Grange, he sets about changing the team’s mindset and tackling his and England’s biggest fear – penalties.

programme.title: BBC Weekend News
programme.desc: The latest national and international news from the BBC.

programme.title: BBC London
programme.desc: The latest news, sport and weather from London.

programme.title: Match of the Day
programme.desc: Highlights of the final day of the Premier League season's ten matches, including Arsenal’s trip to Crystal Palace and Manchester City versus Aston Villa. Elsewhere, Spurs host Everton, and West Ham welcome Leeds to the London Stadium. Presented by Kelly Cates, with Alan Shearer and Wayne Rooney.

programme.title: Football Eras: Andy Robertson meets Kenny Dalglish
programme.desc: Ahead of his final game for Liverpool, Andy Robertson catches up with Sir Kenny Dalglish and BBC Sport's Kelly Cates in a special interview. The left-back reflects on his career at Liverpool and previews Scotland's first World Cup in 28 years.

programme.title: Blankety Blank
programme.desc: The legendary Blankety Blank is back! Bradley Walsh hosts as a panel of six celebrities fill in the missing blanks and help win brilliant prizes for some very lucky contestants. This week, he’s joined by Jonathan Ross, Joanna Page, Harry Redknapp, Joe Swash, Susan Wokoma and Shazia Mirza.

programme.title: Freddie Flintoff's Field of Dreams
programme.desc: Time is running out for sporting legend Freddie Flintoff to help his unlikely cricket team transform, both on and off the field. With the Indian tour reaching its final few days, attention turns to life back in England, and Freddie has his work cut out dealing with thoughts of back home and future fears. Hoping to end the tour on a high, Freddie and the boys head to one of the oldest cricket clubs in India to take on their toughest opponents yet.

programme.title: My Mate's a Bad Date
programme.desc: Georgie’s best mate Vicky is the ultimate dating ice queen. Vicky is super-picky, has a long list of unrealistic criteria for her perfect match, dumps guys for using the wrong emojis, analyses everything they do and walks out of 60 per cent of her dates. Tired of hearing about Vicky’s first date failures and delivering advice that gets ignored, Georgie calls on sex and relationships expert Oloni for an urgent intervention. Oloni wants Vicky to see where it's all going wrong, so she dishes out some cold, hard truths as she unpacks Vicky’s dating history and shows her some frank and honest video feedback from friends, family and even exes, to prove to Vicky that her bad dating behaviour needs a serious reboot. Oloni has to pull out all the stops to melt this ice queen as she sets Vicky up on a series of first dates that will challenge her behaviour and make her a better dater. Under the watchful eye of Oloni and Georgie, is Vicky ready for a change, will she give the guys a chance, be nice and let her guard down, or will she retain her ice queen crown?

programme.title: A Good Girl's Guide to Murder
programme.desc: Pip and Ravi find out more about Andie’s secret life, and when Pip is presented with a new piece of evidence, she reaches a crisis point and questions whether Sal is guilty.

programme.title: A Good Girl's Guide to Murder
programme.desc: Pip discovers more than she bargained for and faces dark consequences when she tries to confront the killer. Just when it seems all is lost, the terrible truth is revealed.

programme.title: The Catch Up
programme.desc: A round-up of the day's news.

programme.title: Deadly 60
programme.desc: Steve heads to Zambia for the first time. He hears hippo communication on a whole new level, spots a high-diving croc and meets some incredible sniffer dogs.

programme.title: Minions
programme.desc: Minions Kevin, Stuart and Bob attend the 1968 Villain-Con, where they land the job of sidekicks to supervillain Scarlet Overkill. She tells them of her evil plans to become Queen of England.

programme.title: Death in Paradise
programme.desc: It’s the team’s most confounding puzzle yet when a passenger boards a flight to Dominica but then seemingly vanishes mid-flight, only to be found back on Saint Marie soil... shot dead. The pilot and flight attendant deny ever seeing the victim on their plane, raising the team’s suspicions. Two further suspects are found back in Saint Marie – it transpires the victim had been caught up in a messy love affair. Neville starts to question whether he’s losing his mind, seriously doubting himself for the first time, until all the pieces of the puzzle finally fit together, revealing the startling truth. Meanwhile, an old face has returned, and Neville has a huge decision to make.

programme.title: Smoggie Queens
programme.desc: There’s no place like home when Dickie takes his work mates on a Wizard of Oz night out.

programme.title: Smoggie Queens
programme.desc: Mam is managing a team for a local charity football match, where Dickie and Lucinda compete to become the best wags!

programme.title: Big Proud Party Agency
programme.desc: Best friends Jordan and Tre ask the agents to come up with a birthday bash, where Jordan also plans a surprise proposal to his boyfriend Corey.

programme.title: Big Proud Party Agency
programme.desc: Best friends Sam and James want to throw one last big party with their friend Xav before they all go their separate ways to uni. Which agent will host their last hurrah?

programme.title: Big Proud Party Agency
programme.desc: Best friends Libby and Jess from Southampton want to arrange an unforgettable 18th birthday party for identical twins Holly and Charlotte, who are Libby’s younger sisters. Despite having already turned 18, the twins never got to celebrate in the proper style due to the pandemic, so the girls are hoping the Big Proud Party Agency can put things right. After hearing what the girls have to say, the party agents compete to win the business. Ryan pitches a VIP glam-up disco with high-fashion style and paparazzi. Christopher whets the appetite with a pool party featuring some half-naked 'mermen', and Teddy wants to set sail on a superyacht before ending the night in a super club. After Libby and Jess choose their favourite party idea, it’s time to get down to business. The winning party agent, supported by the others, heads to Southampton to put their plan into action. The décor has to be hand-picked to suit the theme, performers are auditioned for their very specific talents, and a dance routine is lined up as an extra special surprise. Will the twins get the 18th birthday party they always wanted?

programme.title: Big Proud Party Agency
programme.desc: Best friends Levi and Beth from Cardiff want to organise a class prom for their musical theatre year group who graduated without the normal celebrations due to Covid restrictions. They want the event to centre around leading light Geraint, aka Queen G, who has become noticeably introverted after a homophobic attack left him fearing for his life. After getting their head around the brief, the party agents compete to win the business. Ryan tempts Levi and Beth with his High School Musical-inspired Americana prom. Teddy wants the theme of her party to draw on the 1980s underground ballroom scene - complete with male strippers, and Christopher promises a decadent art deco black and gold affair in a stunning country mansion. After Levi and Beth choose their favourite party idea, it’s time to get down to business. The winning party agent, supported by the others, heads to Cardiff to put their plan into action. A bespoke couture outfit is designed, an amazing ornamental centrepiece is crafted from scratch, and the venue’s doors need to be altered to accommodate a giant glitter ball. Will Geraint rediscover his inner Queen G?

programme.title: Big Proud Party Agency
programme.desc: Best friends Lauren and Tamara from Cheshire are keen to mark the difficult journey of their friend Miya, who underwent gender confirmation surgery a few years ago and has now blossomed into the person she always wanted to be. By celebrating her ‘V Day’, they want to show her how special she is and how much she means to them. After hearing Miya’s incredible story, the party agents compete to win the business. Ryan wants to host a rooftop reception that celebrates transgender visibility. Teddy hopes her three-parties-in-one pitch gets her the gig and Christopher is inspired by butterflies with his 'woodland wilderness' themed event. After Lauren and Tamara choose their favourite party idea, it’s time to get down to business. The winning party agent, supported by the others, heads off to put their plan into action. There are photoshoots to plan, venues to be dressed and red carpets to be rolled out. Will Miya have a ‘V-Day’ to remember?

programme.title: Big Proud Party Agency
programme.desc: Best friends Ryan and Daniel from Belfast want to throw the mother of all comeback shows for their friend and fellow wrestler Shannon. Shannon - aka Skye James, the Rainbow Rebel - injured herself badly performing her signature move nearly two years ago and hasn’t stepped into a ring since. The boys are hoping the Big Proud Party Agency can step up and help her get back through the ropes. After hearing what’s at stake, the party agents compete to win the business. Ryan unveils his 80s arcade-inspired, neon-drenched wrestling smackdown. Teddy campaigns for a rebellion-themed street party, and Christopher wants to create the ultimate fight club in an underground car park. After Ryan and Daniel choose their favourite party idea, it’s time to get down to business. The winning party agent, supported by the others, heads to Belfast to put their plan into action. They need to find a party venue that can accommodate a wrestling ring, design the perfect fighting outfit and taste-test a signature cocktail that will hopefully toast a comeback victory!

programme.title: BBC Comedy Short Films
programme.desc: Brothers, flatmates and polar opposites Samir and Nikhil find themselves on an adventure when they accidentally steal a package from their maniac neighbour Ivan.

programme.title: A Good Girl's Guide to Murder
programme.desc: Pip must come to terms with the repercussions of solving the case. But something isn’t quite right, and Pip puts herself in danger once more to find out the whole truth.

programme.title: Doctor Who (2005–2022)
programme.desc: The Doctor turns bank robber when he is given a task he cannot refuse - steal from the most dangerous bank in the cosmos. With the help of a beautiful shape-shifter and a cyber-augmented gamer, the Doctor and Clara must fight their way past deadly security and come face to face with the fearsome Teller - a creature of terrifying power that can detect guilt.

programme.title: Doctor Who (2005–2022)
programme.desc: When terrifying events threaten Coal Hill School, the Doctor decides to go undercover. The Skovox Blitzer is ready to destroy all humanity - and worse, any second now, Danny Pink and the Doctor are going to meet.

programme.title: Miranda
programme.desc: Since the only man she has ever actually fallen for, Gary, left for Hong Kong, Miranda has done little more than sit in front of the telly with a packet of biscuits and friends made of fruit. Stevie persuades her to move on and so Miranda starts a new regime. She is determined to get fit, lose weight, and become a new woman - the type of woman her boarding school nemesis Tilly and her hard-to-please mother Penny would be proud of. But will she really?

programme.title: Miranda
programme.desc: Miranda is a clumsy woman, but falling in a grave at the end of a funeral service was something not even she was expecting. The experience gets her thinking about what people will say at her funeral, and she wants it to be good, so she signs up a for a charity parachute jump, reads to the elderly and helps her mother Penny and her boarding school nemesis Tilly organise a charity wine tasting event. Hilariously, and much to the embarrassment of everyone around her, her good deeds do not go as well as planned.

programme.title: Mrs Brown's Boys
programme.desc: Agnes Brown's grandson Bono is being bullied by a boy at school. When Agnes makes matters worse, a mediation session is called.

programme.title: Mrs Brown's Boys
programme.desc: When a crime wave hits Finglas, the garda prompts Father Damien and Maria to put together a neighbourhood watch scheme. Agnes and Winnie promptly sign up, but are they really the best people to tackle crime in the area? After Cathy goes on yet another disastrous date, Agnes is determined to put Cathy's man troubles to rest once and for all. Although when they say it's good to 'air your problems', they probably don't mean literally Meanwhile, Betty and Mark lock horns over whether Bono should go to his school disco.

programme.title: The Young Offenders
programme.desc: It’s time for Conor and Jock to try the exciting Cork dating scene when two girls visiting the city catch their attention.

programme.title: The Young Offenders
programme.desc: Mairéad and the lads take the family car on a road trip to pick up her unpredictable mother, but disaster strikes.

programme.title: The Young Offenders
programme.desc: Sun and sand are the new dream, but how can the lads afford it? Enter Billy Murphy with a daring whiskey heist idea.

programme.title: Smoggie Queens
programme.desc: There’s no place like home when Dickie takes his work mates on a Wizard of Oz night out.

programme.title: Smoggie Queens
programme.desc: Mam is managing a team for a local charity football match, where Dickie and Lucinda compete to become the best wags!

programme.title: Two Pints of Lager and a Packet of Crisps
programme.desc: Janet is mugged and decides she wants to become a police community support officer to protect others from the same fate, so sets about learning her policing skills by practising on her friends. Wesley comes back to Runcorn in an attempt to win back Donna's heart and asks Gaz for advice on what to do. Gaz advises Wesley that he needs to be more like him.

programme.title: Two Pints of Lager and a Packet of Crisps
programme.desc: Janet has become a PCSO and tries to gain Gaz's respect by making her first arrest, Louise is worried that Janet is a better mother than her, Donna and Gaz are both distressed when they receive their divorce papers, and Wesley tries to fit in with the gang by learning what he sees as the Runcornian way of life - eating pasties, shimmying like Tim and borrowing each other's clothes.

programme.title: Two Pints of Lager and a Packet of Crisps
programme.desc: Donna and Gaz have decided to get divorced and the decision takes them on a path down memory lane. They look back at their relationship in a series of fantasies and flashbacks, including the time they first met one another at the age of five, where we discover the reason for Gaz's fear of sheep. They also imagine how life would be without one another. Donna wonders what she and Louise will be like as old ladies and Gaz imagines how fat he may have been if they'd never met. This terrifies them and makes them wonder if they have actually made the right decision to divorce.

programme.title: Doctor Who (2005–2022)
programme.desc: The Doctor turns bank robber when he is given a task he cannot refuse - steal from the most dangerous bank in the cosmos. With the help of a beautiful shape-shifter and a cyber-augmented gamer, the Doctor and Clara must fight their way past deadly security and come face to face with the fearsome Teller - a creature of terrifying power that can detect guilt.

programme.desc: When terrifying events threaten Coal Hill School, the Doctor decides to go undercover. The Skovox Blitzer is ready to destroy all humanity - and worse, any second now, Danny Pink and the Doctor are going to meet.
